name: stationary bike run v. 3
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position to ensure proper alignment.
Place your feet on the pedals and secure them with the straps if available.
Start pedaling at a comfortable pace.
Maintain a steady rhythm and increase the resistance as desired.
Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and proper posture.
Continue pedaling for the desired duration of your workout.
Gradually decrease the resistance and slow down before coming to a complete stop.
Stretch your legs and cool down after the workout.

Adjust the seat height and position to ensure proper alignment. Place your feet on the pedals and secure them with the straps if available. Start pedaling at a comfortable pace. Maintain a steady rhythm and increase the resistance as desired. Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and proper posture. Continue pedaling for the desired duration of your workout. Gradually decrease the resistance and slow down before coming to a complete stop. Stretch your legs and cool down after the workout.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
